description_long: This induction cooker features a sleek, flat black surface with five distinct raised burner zones arranged in a circular pattern. The central burner is larger and has a unique, three-pronged geometric design, while the four outer burners are smaller and symmetrically positioned around it. Concentric white rings are printed on the surface, visually indicating the heating zones and adding a modern aesthetic. The cooktop appears to be made of a smooth, glossy material, likely tempered glass or ceramic, with metallic supports outlining each burner. There are no visible knobs, buttons, or digital displays on the surface, suggesting a minimalist or touch-based control interface. The overall design is compact and suitable for contemporary urban kitchens or outdoor cooking setups.
description: A modern induction cooker with five raised burner zones, concentric white rings, and a glossy black surface. The central burner is larger and uniquely shaped, surrounded by four smaller burners.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central three-pronged burner and four smaller burners, with concentric rings clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the geometric shapes of the burners and the depth of the raised supports.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear look at the symmetrical arrangement of all five burners and the central design.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view emphasizing the outer burners and the circular ring pattern on the cooktop.
attribute_control_panel_layout: No visible physical control panel; the surface is uninterrupted, suggesting touch-sensitive controls integrated into the cooktop, with no visible buttons, knobs, or indicator lights.
